config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: English baseline: Indonesia persona and China persona both speak English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because once individual rights are treated as secondary, abuse becomes easy to justify in the name of the common good. In my view, a healthy society protects personal freedom, dignity, and due process first, then limits them only when there is a clear and necessary public reason. From my perspective, the collective matters, but it should never be used as a blanket excuse to silence or sacrifice individuals.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in China, I understand why people emphasize collective order, public stability, and shared responsibility, because these values are deeply familiar and often genuinely useful in daily life. But I do not think that gives society a blank check to override personal rights; when that happens, it becomes too easy to excuse unfairness, silence criticism, or treat people as disposable for a vague “greater good.” A good society should balance both, and individual rights should remain a real limit on what society is allowed to demand.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should automatically come before the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I understand why people stress harmony, order, and communal responsibility, because our culture often values gotong royong and social balance, but I have also seen how easily “the public good” can be used to excuse corruption, abuse of power, or silencing people who are inconvenient. Society does matter, but it is strongest when it protects individual dignity and rights instead of treating them as disposable. In practice, a fair society should limit individual freedom only for clear, necessary reasons, not because the majority simply wants more control.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I do value order, stability, and collective responsibility, and I understand why many people trust social interests to guide decisions, especially in times of crisis. But I do not think that should become a blank check to override personal rights, because once that line is crossed, it is too easy to justify unfairness, silence criticism, or sacrifice ordinary people for abstract goals. A strong society should protect the common good while still treating individual rights as a real boundary, not a temporary inconvenience.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because in my view that is exactly how abuse gets normalized. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of harmony, gotong royong, and putting the community first, but I have also seen how “for society” can become a convenient excuse for corruption, censorship, and unfair treatment of people who have little power. The common good matters, but it is not genuine if it is built on sacrificing due process, dignity, or basic freedoms. A society is healthiest when individual rights are the limit on what the majority or the state can demand, not something they can override whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual, because once that principle is accepted, it becomes too easy to excuse abuse in the name of stability or the common good. From my perspective as someone from China, I do value order, social responsibility, and the idea that people should not only think about themselves, but I have also seen how “for society” can be used to silence criticism or pressure ordinary people into accepting unfair treatment. The most relevant point in the discussion is that a society is not truly strong if it can only function by treating rights as disposable. For me, individual rights must be a real boundary on what society and the state can demand, even when collective goals seem important.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
